abstract.para: You can check the status and configuration of existing cluster resources with the &crmshell; (&crmsh;) or with the &hawk; Web interface.

step.para: Log in either as the &rootuser; user or as a user with sudo privileges.
Show the status of the resources:
Show the configuration of the resources. The basic command shows the full CIB:
You can limit the output by using modifiers such as an object's name, type or tag. For more information about the available modifiers, run the following command:
In a Web browser, go to https://HAWKSERVER:7630/ and log in.
The Status page shows the cluster resources and their statuses. If the resource is a group or a clone, you can click the plus icon to show the child resources.
From the left navigation bar, select Configuration Edit Configuration.
On this page, you can click different tabs to view more details about cluster objects such as resources, constraints or nodes. You can also limit the results by using the Search bar.
To view the full CIB, click Show.
Alternatively, you can click XML to view the XML representation of the CIB.
